target: next best finite-level r=3 determinant and compact-factorization triage
scope: Advisory Only planning for the next finite-level r=3 research cycle, starting from existing exact S-level characteristic-polynomial closures at s = 0.50, 0.55, and 0.60.
out_of_scope: global Collatz conjecture proof; any claim that finite spectral closure implies global orbit behavior; any transfer of r=2 mechanisms to r=3 without an independent r=3 witness
next_best_steps: Run the Tier 1 Python reconnaissance bundle to refresh r=3 spectral, modular determinant, factor-search, and cross-level observations.
Collect a witness manifest immediately after the Python run so every new report artifact is hash-addressed.
Promote only stable or contradictory patterns to Sage exact algebra, starting with sage-r3-unit; run sage-r3-full only if the unit result gives a concrete factor/determinant target.
Audit every generated report with the proof-boundary validator before updating canonical notes or Lean stubs.
If exact Sage produces a stable theorem candidate, generate a theorem-candidate note first; Lean stubs come only after the proof auditor classifies the claim as finite-level and exact.

subtasks.task: Inspect r=3 unit/full determinant and factorization hypotheses after refreshed Python probes. Search for exact symbolic structures, factorization hypotheses, quotient/residual decompositions, and invariant decompositions relevant to compact-factorization triage.
subtasks.backend: Sage exact algebra if determinant/charpoly/rank/kernel is involved; otherwise Python exact prototypes.

subtasks.task: Run bounded reconnaissance first: spectral-fast, modular-unit-fast, modular-full-fast, factor-python, then cross-level. Produce JSON/log artifacts and preserve return codes.
subtasks.backend: Python orchestration with Sage when exact algebra is required.

subtasks.task: Classify all outputs using the claim ladder and block Collatz-level escalation.
subtasks.backend: report-only plus optional Lean stub generation.

subtasks.task: Patch scripts/tests if the target exposes missing harness functionality.

expected_artifacts: proof-boundary validator JSON printed to stdout, saved if the next execution pass needs a durable audit artifact
claim_boundary: This is a finite-level structural/spectral/determinant planning report inside the lifted-operator framework. It does not prove or imply the Collatz conjecture.
blocked_items: python is not available as a command in the current shell. This pass used /tmp/codex-python-shim/python -> /usr/bin/python3; either keep that shim in the command environment or add a real Python alias before running repo scripts that call python internally.
Sage exact work is not scheduled until the Python reconnaissance produces a concrete target or the user explicitly asks to spend the exact-algebra runtime.
next_step: Run the Tier 1 command sequence, collect the witness manifest, and audit the generated reports. If refreshed observations show a stable determinant/factor pattern, promote the smallest exact target to sage-r3-unit.
subagent_spawn_prompt: Spawn four subagents: algebra_explorer, experiment_runner, proof_auditor, and implementation_engineer. Have each return commands run, files touched, artifacts, claim labels, and blockers. Consolidate only after all results return.
